markdownRemark.html:
In the development of component libraries or specific pages, it is necessary to restore the style according to the specific design draft, and the restoration process needs to follow the specifications, otherwise the restored page may have incompatible styles under some themes or 'layout', or the layout may be incorrect after modifying the browser's default font.

Therefore, it is recommended to refer to the following precautions when restoring the style:

Layout related

  1. Use flex layouts sparingly

    • Flex layouts can be unusual when the content is multi-row
    • Also 'display: flex; ' and 'text-overflow: ellipsis; ' Setting to the same element is invalid and needs to be set in a child element of the flex layout element, otherwise it will affect the style of the content overflow
    • For example: Button uses a flex layout to center the content, which will cause overflow ··· Styles are invalid. Modifying the display property of an element can also introduce some potential problems

  2. Attributes such as padding / border affect page layout

    • If there is a difference in size between the old and new styles, such as adding padding / border, it may likely affect the layout in actual use, causing misalignment and other problems, and multi-scenario testing is required after modification.

Style Prioritization

  1. When using :not() selector, pay attention to the priority impact and try to avoid using it

    • The selectors excluded in :not() will increase the overall priority, make the priority unstable, and prone to the problem of style overlay confusion.
  2. Use the attribute selector sparingly, paying attention to the scope of influence
  3. For example: abstract selectors like [class*="xxx"] are very unstable and may lead to excessive style overrides, causing style anomalies.

  4. Ways to override priority

    • To override the necessary styles, increase the priority by one unit for the corresponding class name only. Do not increase it at the root level, as that will raise the priority for all styles.
    • For fixed special styles, you can use !important.

  5. Write the standard state styles for input-type components in sequential order (following the order can reduce some unnecessary priority overrides), for example: normal >> required >> invalid >> disabled

    // normal
.input-class {
  color: black;
  // required
  &-required {
     border-color: yellow;
  }
  // invalid
  &-invalid {
     color: red;
  }
  // disabled
  &-disabled {
     color: black;
     border-color: black;
     background-color: gray;
  }
}

Additional Effects of Properties

  1. Some CSS properties have additional effects that need attention when used:

    • pointer-events: affects mouse events; improper use may cause mouse events to stop working
    • touch-action: affects how touch gestures interact with the element
    • font / letter-spacing / word-spacing: affects content width calculations in the component library
    • word-wrap: allows content to wrap at appropriate positions depending on the scenario
    • ::before / ::after: pseudo-elements are included in scrollWidth

Notes on Unit Usage

Background: Standardizing style unit usage ensures compatibility of component and page styles when changing the base font size.

  • The main units used in the component library are px and rem. px is a fixed pixel unit, and rem is a relative unit.
  • Use px for elements that require fixed dimensions according to the visual design, such as 1px lines or 4px rounded borders. If the visual designer specifies that a border must be 2px, then it is fixed.
  • The rem unit is relative to the root element () font size and is suitable for layout elements that need to adapt based on screen size, such as overall page layout, navigation menus, buttons, etc.
  • Use rem for font sizes and most spacing.

Below are most cases for style unit usage:

1. px

  • border-width
  • border-radius
  • padding
  • margin
  • outline

Special cases:

  • Width in media queries, e.g.: @media (min-width:600px) {}
  • Fixed design widths and heights
  • Fixed design fonts: font-size: 13px;
  • Width and height in responsive layouts using px

2. rem

  • height
  • width
  • font-size
  • top
  • bottom
  • left
  • right
  • letter-spacing

Special cases: Some margins use rem: margin, padding

3. vw, vh

vw (Viewport Width) and vh (Viewport Height) are units based on the viewport (browser display area). Other units based on the viewport include vmin and vmax.

  • vw: 1vw equals 1% of the viewport width
  • vh: 1vh equals 1% of the viewport height
  • vmin: takes the smaller of vw and vh, so in mobile portrait mode, 1vmin = 1vw
  • vmax: takes the larger of vw and vh, so in mobile portrait mode, 1vmax = 1vh

Usage scenarios:

  • These four units are commonly used in mobile development to adapt to different phones and screen sizes. They are less commonly used on PC and should be avoided when possible.
  • On PC, some layout-related styles or responsive layouts may require their use, but in projects, you should check how they display on browsers of different sizes to ensure compatibility.

4. em

  • em is a relative length unit. em is similar to rem, but rem is based on the font size of the root element, while em is based on the font size of the parent element. If the font size of the current inline text is not manually set, it will be relative to the browser's default font size.
  • It is not recommended to use em as a unit unless the use case is very clear and the relationship of length units is well understood.

5. Others

  • line-height

    1. Multiples are preferred, adjusted according to the actual situation;
    2. If the font uses rem, use rem or multiples;
    3. If the font uses px, use px or multiples;
  • If a container is fixed using px, be cautious when using rem inside; px is preferred.
  • When calculating between different units, use calc to wrap them, for example: calc(0.3rem + 10px)
  • For calculations between less variables and specific values, it is also recommended to use calc (Note: when compiling less, calculations between variables with different units will add the values directly and adopt the unit of the first operand. For example: 0.3rem + 10px will be compiled as 10.3rem).

frontmatter.title: 组件常见唯一 Key 场景说明
Explanation of Common Unique Key Scenarios for Components
设计&样式还原开发注意事项
